name: Fresh Coconut Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Puncture eyes of coconut.
2. Drain liquid and reserve.
3. Place coconut in a preheated oven at 350°F for 20 minutes.
4. Remove shells using a hammer or blunt knife.
5. Pare and chunk the coconut.
6. In a blender jar, whirl half cup of coconut with reserved coconut liquid about 30 seconds until well grated.
7. Remove grated coconut; repeat with remaining coconut.
8. Combine 3 cups grated coconut, reserving 1 cup for Seven Minute Frosting.
9. Grease and lightly flour two 8-inch round cake pans.
10. Cream together 3/4 cup sugar, shortening, and lemon extract until light and fluffy.
11. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a separate bowl.
12. Beat one-third of the dry ingredients into the creamed mixture.
13. Add one-half cup milk; repeat with remaining dry ingredients.
14. Fold in 1 cup coconut.
15. Beat egg whites to a foamy stage.
16. Gradually add remaining sugar, beating until stiff peaks form.
17. Gently fold egg white mixture into the batter.
18. Pour batter into prepared cake pans.
19. Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
20. Cool cakes on wire racks for 5 minutes before removing from pans.
21. Frost one layer with Seven Minute Coconut Frosting; sprinkle top with 1/4 cup coconut.
22. Add second layer and frost entire cake.
23. Sprinkle remaining coconut over the top and sides.
SEVEN MINUTE COCONUT FROSTING
1. Combine egg whites, sugar, cream of tartar, cold water, and lemon extract in the top of a double boiler.
2. Beat mixture for 1 minute.
3. Set the top over boiling water; cook at high speed for 7 minutes or until stiff peaks form.
4. Remove from heat; fold in reserved grated coconut.
